rulings.Case Title: EST. OF DONALD EUGENE CHERRY
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, Appointment Under IAEA
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 02/18/2025 (no earlier date available). The following issues must be addressed re the amended petition filed on 01/02/2025 and DE-121 form filed on 01/08/2025: 1) The word "AMENDED" must be inserted at item 2 of the DE-121 form. 2) Christian Cherry must be removed from items 2 & 3 of the DE-121 form. 3) Item 4a of proof of service attached to the DE-121 form shows 01/08/2025 as the date of mailing, less than 15 days before this hearing. New notice of the continued hearing must be given with a copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(C)(6) attached thereto. 4) Exhibit B attached to the supplement filed on 01/08/2025 indicates that Lanoo Cherry and Neneh Cherry are citizens of Sweden, in which case notice of the amended petition must be given to the Swedish Consulate. Probate Code section 8113. If Christian Cherry is a citizen of Denmark, notice must also be given to the Danish Consulate.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ROBERT A. CHASE
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 3/19/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) If the addresses for Darcie Quinn and the Berkeley Repertory Theatre were correct in the petition, notice was mailed to them again at the wrong addresses. (2) When an accounting is presented, ORIGINAL bank or brokerage statements must be filed to verify the ending balance(s) shown in the accounting. See CRC 7.575(b) for computer-generated printouts. (3) If the costs shown on Exhibit B to the petition were already paid out of the estate, why are they still shown as a liability due to the executor? (4) Likewise, the disbursements schedule shows most of the payments reportedly paid by the attorney out of the "cost retainer." Were they paid by the estate or the attorney? (5) Exhibit K also shows a check for $88.76 described as "Estate of Chase," which is not a helpful description. (6) Refunds for any amounts paid out of the estate are not includable in the fee base. Please confirm that the refund amounts on 7/5/23 and 3/11/24 were not for amounts paid out of the estate. (7) Interest on the pecuniary bequests are not reflected on Schedule J of the amendment (proposed distribution). The daily interest on $10,000 is $1.92, so, for the 190 days until just 12/31/24, would have been $364.80 to each. Deadline for providing the assigned examiner courtesy copies of any new filed papers is 3/5/25. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (A petition should be amended or supplemented by the petitioner, as appropriate, or missing papers should simply be delivered to the court; there is no need to "respond" to issues, whether they are in formal notes or posted in a tentative ruling.) Except for exhibits, each paper filed with the court must bear a footer in the bottom margin of each page, placed below the page number and divided from the rest of the document page by a printed line. [CRC 2.110(a).]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MONSERRATE MAGOBET
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Please have the CASE NUMBER and the CASE NAME ready for the clerk. Parties appearing must check in for the hearing using their FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear in person or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. Please review LRSF 14.1 D. for information on remote appearances. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 160 225 4765 and password is 514879. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878.

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: DURABLE POWER OF ATTORNEY OF CARMEN E. BROWN
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION Petition For 1. Breach Of Fiduciary Duties By Former Attorney In Fact; 2. Financial Elder Abuse; 3. Compel Accoutning By Former Attorney In Fact; And 4. Attorney'S Fees And Costs
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ANDREW CHU
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 4/2/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No guardian ad litem has been appointed yet for the minor heirs. New ex parte petitions were filed on 12/23/24; however, courtesy copies were just received by the assigned examiner. (2) In any event, notice will have to be given to (or waived by) the guardian ad litem once he/she is appointed. (3) Notice is defective if mailed before the pleading requiring notice is e-filed. The amended petition was not e-filed until 12/3/24. Notice mailed on 11/26/24 showed on its face that the [amended] petition "has been filed," which it was not. (4) A partner who is not a registered domestic partner does not have standing before heirs or the public administrator. [Probate Code Section 8461.] Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 3/19/25. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MURRAY MUHAMMAD
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 2/18/25 to address the following issues: 1) The Proof of Service filed on 12/6/24 is incomplete. Please make sure that all items under "Proof of Service by Mail" is completed by the person who provided service and refile both the Notice of Hearing and the Proof of Service. 2) A completed proposed order must be filed. All documents to be filed by 2/3/25. Petitioner should include their email address to the pleadings that are filed.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE HARRY W. MOSS TRUST DATED FEBRUARY 20, 2024 TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Confirming Trust Property
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 4/2/25. (No earlier availability.) 1. The amended petition was not e-filed until 12/12/24. Notice is defective if mailed before the pleading requiring notice is e-filed. [LRSF 14.14 A.] 2. Robert Bohle is missing from the list of beneficiaries. Did he predecease the trustor? 3. The trustor apparently had no issue and his parents presumably predeceased him. The petitioner does not address this or identify the parents and state their dates of death. (Heirs who are not beneficiaries would be entitled to personal service of notice. Probate Code Section 851.) Deadline to deliver courtesy copies of any responsive papers to the assigned examiner is 3/19/25. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ROBERT EDWARD HAYWOOD
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 3/19/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No proof of publication of notice has been filed. (2) The petition caption is incomplete. (3) The Notice of Petition to Administer Estate is also incomplete at item 5, if IAEA authority is being requested. (4) The instructions for how to appear remotely were only served on 1/6/25, which was not at least 15 days before this hearing. (Since the decedent's father is the only other heir, he could waive notice.) (It appears that the proposed order was just lodged on 1/7/25.) Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 3/5/25. A supplement to the petition, prepared on pleading paper and signed and verified by the petitioner, must be filed to address the issue of the incomplete caption. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.
